title: Diplodoc (presets.yaml)

default.description: Default preset — always applied to every build regardless of varsPreset.
default.markdownDescription:
**`object`** Default preset — **always applied** to every build regardless of the `varsPreset` setting.

Define your baseline variables here. Other presets are deep-merged on top.

```yaml
default:
  version: '2.0'
  product_name: My Docs
```

i18n.description: Per-language variable overrides. Keys are language codes, values are variable maps.
i18n.markdownDescription:
**`object`** Per-language variable overrides.

Keys are language codes (e.g. `en`, `ru`). Each value is a map of variable names to their values for that language.

Variable resolution order: `i18n[currentLang]` → preset root → `default` preset.

preset.description: A named set of template variables. Variables are accessible as {{ var_name }} in Markdown and toc.yaml.
preset.markdownDescription:
**`object`** A named set of template variables.

Variables are available in Liquid expressions as `{{ var_name }}`.

The special `i18n` key holds per-language variable overrides:
```yaml
i18n:
  en:
    greeting: Hello
  ru:
    greeting: Привет
```

property.description: Preset variable value: string, number, or nested object.
property.markdownDescription:
**`string | number | object`** Preset variable value.

- **string** — text value, supports Markdown inline syntax (`translate: md`)
- **number** — numeric value
- **object** — nested variable namespace (access as `{{ parent.child }}`)

markdownDescription:
Diplodoc presets file (`presets.yaml`).
Defines named sets of template variables used in Liquid expressions inside `.md` files and `toc.yaml`.
The `default` preset is always applied. Additional presets are activated via `varsPreset` in `.yfm` or the `--vars-preset` CLI flag.
```yaml default:
  version: '2.0'
  env: production

internal:
  env: internal
  show_drafts: true

i18n:
  en:
    greeting: Hello
  ru:
    greeting: Привет
```
